BusinessName.BusinessNameLine2Txt: DBA IMPACT DRUG AND ALCOHOL TREATMENT

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TREATMENT OF ALCOHOL AND DRUG ADDICTION.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TREATMENT OF ALCOHOL AND DRUG ADDICTION.
IRS990.Desc: RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT PROGRAMS: SERVICES PROVIDED INCLUDED PHYSICAL AND EMOTIONAL THERAPY, VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION AND TRAINING, AND GUIDANCE DESIGNED TO HELP THOSE WITH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: OUTPATIENT TREATMENT AND TRANSITIONAL LIVING FOR THOSE WITH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER: SERVICES PROVIDED INCLUDE GROUP AND INDIVIDUAL THERAPY, 12 STEP MEETINGS, URINALYSIS TESTING, VOCATIONAL COUNSELING AND TRAINING AND GUIDANCE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IMPACT FOLLOWS THE PROVISIONS OF FASB ASC 740, ACCOUNTING FOR UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES ("ASC 740"), WHICH PRESCRIBES A MINIMUM RECOGNITION THRESHOLD AND MEASUREMENT METHODOLOGY FOR TAX POSITIONS TAKEN, OR EXPECTED TO BE TAKEN, IN A TAX RETURN PRIOR TO RECOGNITION IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. ASC 740 ALSO PROVIDES GUIDANCE FOR DERECOGNITION, CLASSIFICATION, INTEREST, PENALTIES, AND ACCOUNTING IN INTERIM PERIODS AND DISCLOSURE. TAX BENEFITS ARE RECORDED ONLY FOR TAX POSITIONS THAT ARE MORE-LIKELY-THAN-NOT TO BE SUSTAINED UPON EXAMINATION BY TAX AUTHORITIES. MANAGEMENT DOES NOT BELIEVE THERE WERE ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS AS OF JUNE 30, 2024. IMPACT IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAX UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF SECTION 501 (C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE, AND CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE SECTION 23701D. ACCORDINGLY, A PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES IS NOT INCLUDED IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. IMPACT BELIEVES THAT IT HAS APPROPRIATE SUPPORT FOR THE POSITIONS TAKEN ON ITS TAX RETURNS AND THE EXEMPT STATUS WOULD BE SUSTAINED ON EXAMINATION. IMPACT CLASSIFIES INTEREST AND PENALTIES ON UNDERPAYMENTS OF INCOME TAX (UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME TAX), IF APPLICABLE, AS INTEREST EXPENSE AND PENALTIES, RESPECTIVELY, BOTH OF WHICH WOULD BE INCLUDED IN GENERAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE EXPENSES. IMPACT DID NOT INCUR ANY PENALTIES AND INTEREST IN THE CURRENT YEAR. IMPACT FILES INFORMATIONAL RETURNS IN THE U.S. FEDERAL AND CALIFORNIA STATE JURISDICTIONS.

BusTrInvolveInterestedPrsnGrp.RelationshipDescriptionTxt: FORMER EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, CURRENT BOARD MEMBER

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SENIOR ACCOUNTING MANAGER PERFORMS THE FIRST, DETAILED REVIEW OF THE DRAFT OF THE FORM 990. ANY CORRECTIONS/ADJUSTMENTS ARE IDENTIFIED, AND A FINAL DRAFT OF THE FORM 990 IS THEN REVIEWED BY THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, CONTROLLER AND ASSISTANT CONTROLLER PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE RETURN.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, TRUSTEES, AND KEY EMPLOYEES RECEIVED A COPY OF THE POLICY AND THEY ARE REQUIRED TO DISCLOSE ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST TO THE ORGANIZATION. THE BOARD REVIEWS AND DISCUSSES ANY CONFLICTS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR'S SALARY IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED ANNUALLY BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: NO GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC.
NO CHANGE IN THE PROCESS FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.
